threat_model: A collected operator shadow-pilot observation receipt could be mistaken for runtime promotion approval if it permits authority flags, promotion-ready scenario claims, or closure claims. The mitigation is a schema-backed local-only receipt with hard false authority fields, remaining AwaitingEvidence promotion gates, and focused tests for authority, gate, and collection-state drift.

required_checks.check: authority boundary check proves the observation receipt keeps runtime promotion, execution, dispatch, runtime replanning, success claims, and terminal closure false
promotion gate check proves runtime approval, replay/recovery, and terminal closure gates remain required after local observations are collected
receipt integrity check proves the checked-in fixture matches the deterministic produced receipt and validates against the schema
audit visibility check records one collected local observation for every expected shadow dossier plan class

findings.mitigation: The schema and validator hard-deny runtime_promotion_authorized, execution_allowed, dispatch_allowed, runtime_replanning_enabled, success_claim_allowed, and terminal_closure.
The receipt requires three remaining AwaitingEvidence promotion gates after local observations are collected.

mitigations: keep the observation receipt local, deterministic, and read-only
hard-deny all runtime promotion and terminal closure fields in the schema
bind the receipt to the deterministic evidence request id and shadow dossier id
record collected scenario observations without claiming runtime promotion readiness
